description: Baseline linear Y (solid taper from 0.5um to 2.5um over 10um, abrupt split to two 0.5um WGs, 2um center separation).
1x2 MMI W=3.0um L=4.2um, cosine-eased tapered access arms, 200nm center trench.
MMI W=2.4um L=5.3um (symmetric 1x2 formula L=3*Lpi/8), same access arms.
Added 0.5um linear input taper 0.5->1.0um before MMI.
Input taper exit widened to 1.2um - WORSE, discarded.
Arm root narrowed to 0.7um by widening center trench - MUCH WORSE.
Footprint fix: device in [0,10]. Arms shortened to 3um (was 5um) - metric dropped.
Parabolic input taper (t^2) - worse than linear.
Adiabatic Y-junction (single polygon, slot at x=3.5) - WORSE than MMI.
Narrower MMI W=2.2 L=4.2, longer taper=1.8, arms=4.0.
Narrower MMI W=2.0 L=3.5 taper=2.5 - slight drop vs W=2.2.
Cosine-eased input taper - slightly worse than linear.
W=2.3 L=4.4 taper=1.6 - worse than W=2.2.
MMI output taper to 1.8um - breaks self-imaging, WORSE.
Narrow MMI W=2.1 L=3.6 taper=1.8 arms=4.6.
Sine arm profile - much worse than cosine.
Final validation - best design (W=2.1 L=3.65 Lt=1.5 wt=0.95, grid 30/wvl). 99.66% transmission, 0.015dB loss.
